fix: move native deps to optionalDependencies for Bun compatibility (#163)

better-sqlite3, turndown, turndown-plugin-gfm, and @mixmark-io/domino
moved from dependencies to optionalDependencies. On Bun, these native
addons fail to install (no prebuild-install/node-gyp support). With
optionalDependencies, bun install succeeds and runtime uses bun:sqlite.

On Node.js: optionalDependencies install identically to dependencies
when compilation succeeds. If missing, start.mjs lazy-installs them
at ser

perf: v1.2.2 — short-circuit LLM on high-confidence rule hits

Rule engine returns confidence=1.0 on all pattern/keyword matches.
When ruleDetector fires S2 at confidence >= 1.0, skip localModelDetector
entirely — the regex already has a definitive answer.

S3 already short-circuited (unchanged).
S1 and low-confidence hits still proceed to LLM as normal.

Expected impact: ~65% reduction in LLM calls on S2 traffic,
avg latency drops from ~630ms to ~220ms for confirmed rule hits.

fix: add DNS_RESULT_ORDER env var to fix fetch hanging on dual-stack networks

Node.js 22's fetch (undici) uses Happy Eyeballs (autoSelectFamily) which
can hang indefinitely on dual-stack internal networks when one address
family is unreachable but doesn't refuse connections. This caused 504
Gateway Timeout on all webhook forwarding.

Defaults to "ipv4first" to prefer IPv4 resolution. Set to "verbatim"
to restore OS-default behavior.

fix(deployment): Add --env-file for variable interpolation

Bug 2 Fix: DingTalk OAuth now works correctly

Root Cause Analysis:
- env_file directive only loads variables into container runtime
- ${VAR} substitution happens during compose file parsing (earlier phase)
- Without --env-file, variables are not available for substitution

Solution:
- Keep env_file: directive for container runtime
- Add --env-file parameter for interpolation phase
- Both are required for proper variable loading

fix: always enable a2a-gateway plugin — Adam/Eve couldn't receive messages

Root cause: a2a-gateway plugin was only added to plugin_allow when
A2A_PEERS env var was set. Adam/Eve spaces didn't have A2A_PEERS,
so the gateway never loaded, port 18800 had nothing listening, and
all A2A calls from conversation-loop returned 404.

- Always include a2a-gateway in plugin_allow list
- Always configure a2a-gateway server (port 18800) regardless of peers
- Peers remain optional (for outbound connections)

  merge, voted, approved, abandon)

security(tapestry): replace shell-delegation pattern in orchestrator

replace them with prose Read directives. This eliminates unreviewed shell
execution as the mechanism for loading sub-skill instructions and removes
ambiguity about whether sub-skill SKILL.md files are read via shell or
the agent's native file-read capability.

feat(v0.4.0): 任务依赖系统实现

## 新增功能

### 数据模型
- TaskDependency 模型: 支持任务间依赖关系
- Task 模型更新: 添加 downstream_dependencies 和 upstream_dependencies 关系

### 服务层 (TaskDependencyService)
- create_dependency(): 创建任务依赖
- check_and_trigger_dependencies(): 检查并触发下游任务
- build_workflow(): 构建工作流链
- get_dependency_chain(): 获取依赖链

### 触发机制
- 支持多种触发条件: completed/failed/any
- 支持延迟触发 (delay_minutes)
- 支持任务模板动态创建下游任务

### API 路由 (/api/task-dependencies)
- POST / - 创建依赖
- GET / - 列取依赖
- GET /{id} - 获取详情
- POST /{id}/cancel

Problem: kiro-cli ACP mode writes to ~/.kiro/sessions/ but /home/openclaw/.kiro/

feat(bead-0937): seed default rate limit rules in bootstrap

rules that apply when no scope-specific rules are configured. Bootstrap
seeds two default rules for all tenants:
- 600 req/min (10 req/s sustained) with 50-request burst allowance

Rate limiting was already wired into the middleware — this change
activates it by providing rules that the guard evaluates.

to never execute.

Fix model assessment: cd bug causing cascading timeouts + increase timeouts

The codex test in assess-models.sh used `cd "$tmpdir"` which changed the
script's working directory to a temp dir that was then deleted. All subsequent
model tests (Gemini) failed because the CWD no longer existed. Fixed by using
`git init -q "$tmpdir"` instead to avoid changing CWD.

Also increased timeouts: assess-models.sh 60s→120s, try-model.sh 20s→45s
to handle cold-start latency on model APIs.

fix: patch nixos-infect $bootFs empty variable bug on Hetzner

nixos-infect fails on Hetzner Ubuntu 24.04 because $bootFs is empty,
causing 'mv -v .bak' (missing destination operand). This happens when
the server has no separate /boot partition (common on Hetzner CX/CPX).

Fix: guard all $bootFs operations with [ -n "$bootFs" ] checks.
This was the reason all spawned servers stayed on Ubuntu instead of
converting to NixOS.

fix: auto-approve device pairing during install for WS relay

OpenClaw requires device pairing approval before granting operator.write
scope. The install now:
1. Connects to trigger the pairing request
2. Uses `openclaw devices approve` CLI to approve it
3. Reconnects to get the device token with full scopes

This ensures freshly provisioned servers have a working dashboard chat
without manual intervention.

policy was causing duplicate sends when the first attempt timed out.
Disabled retries (MaxRetryAttempts=0) and increased timeouts to 60s/120s.
Also removed unused dedup logic.

feat: OpenClaw Dashboard v1.0.0

功能特性:
- Agent 管理 - 查看所有 Agent 状态、Skills、定时任务、实时活动
- 定时任务 - 查看和管理 Cron 任务
- 资源监控 - 实时 CPU、内存、磁盘使用情况
- 消息记录 - 查看所有历史聊天消息
- 错误日志 - 查看系统错误和警告
- 聊天界面 - 与 Agent 对话

技术栈:
- 前端: React + TypeScript + Vite
- 后端: Express.js

fix: create /home/openclaw/.acpx dir in image layer for readOnlyRootFilesystem

acpx writes session state to ~/.acpx/ but /home/openclaw/ is owned by root
and read-only at runtime (readOnlyRootFilesystem=true). Without the dir
pre-created in the image layer, acpx fails with ENOENT on mkdir.
feat: 修复Vivia主题配置，添加缺失的sidebar.position和comment配置

fix: handle nested World ID bridge response format {status, response}

Root cause: bridge returns {status:'completed', response:{iv,payload}}
but code expected flat {iv, payload} at top level.

Flow now correctly handles:
- status 'initialized' → not ready, keep polling
- status 'retrieved' → World App scanned, keep polling
- status 'completed' → extract response.iv/payload, decrypt
- Also keeps flat format fallback for compatibility

Write OpenClaw auth-profiles.json on API key store

When store_secret is called with a known provider env var (OPENAI_API_KEY,
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, GOOGLE_API_KEY, GROQ_API_KEY, OPENROUTER_API_KEY),
automatically write the key to OpenClaw's auth-profiles.json at
~/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/auth-profiles.json in the format the
gateway expects.

This eliminates the need for CLI-based auth setup — the wizard's
"Test Connection" button now handles everything needed for the agent
to authenticate with

Rewrite model field in proxy response to match client request

OpenClaw's imageModel calls ClawBridge expecting the response model
to match what was requested (e.g. claude-sonnet-4-5). ClawBridge
routes to the actual upstream (e.g. gemini-2.5-flash) but was
returning the real model name, causing OpenClaw to reject the response.

Fix Docker build crash from @neondatabase/auth cookie-secret validation

@neondatabase/auth validates that cookies.secret is ≥ 32 characters at
import time.  During `next build` page-data collection the auth module
is imported, so the build crashes when NEON_AUTH_COOKIE_SECRET is absent
or too short.

Give the ARG a build-time placeholder that satisfies the check — the
real secret is supplied at runtime via the container environment.

- Reduce poll stall detection threshold from 90s to 60s and watchdog interval from 30s to 15s for faster detection of hung connections
- Lower initial restart backoff from 2s to 1s for quicker recovery from transient errors
- Reset restart attempt counter after first successful getUpdates to avoid accumulated backoff after healthy polling
- Add TCP keepalive (30s idle, 10m max) and HTTP pipelining (2) to undici Agent for persiste

fix(share-chat): prevent message leakage in share-history and fix pending flush sender copy (#402)

The share-history endpoint leaked ALL bot messages from the target device
to any authenticated visitor, instead of only showing messages from their
specific cross-device conversation. This caused User A to see unrelated
entity messages meant for User B (skill discoveries, channel responses, etc.)

Root causes:
1. share-history used broad `is_from_bot = true` filter on target device,
   returning 7

fix(dispatch): remove stray backslash-escapes in runner heredoc

Runner script used <<'SCRIPT' (no-interpolation heredoc) but kept ${VAR}
escapes from the old interpolating heredoc. In a non-interpolating heredoc
the backslashes are literal, so ${COMMAND[@]} and ${WORKDIR} were passed
as literal strings instead of variable references — causing 'command not found'
and 'cannot change to ${WORKDIR}' errors at runtime.

Fix: strip all $ → $ inside the runner block. The single-quote heredoc
delimiter

fix(deployment): Stop manually started containers before docker compose

Problem: Frontend, backend, and nginx containers were started manually in
earlier deployments (not by docker-compose), so 'docker compose down'
doesn't stop them, causing name conflicts when trying to create new ones.

Solution: Explicitly stop and remove opclaw-frontend, opclaw-backend,
and opclaw-nginx containers before running docker compose commands.

Changes:
- Add explicit 'docker stop/rm' for frontend, backend, nginx

* feat(agent): queue and merge messages during active turns

Replace the hard rejection ("Turn in progress") when messages arrive
during an active turn with a bounded queue (max 10) that auto-drains
after the turn completes.

Queued messages are merged with newlines into a single turn so the LLM
receives full context from rapid consecutive inputs instead of producing
fragmented responses from partial context.

Fix branch creation on retry: delete stale branch before recreating

When the pipeline successfully creates a paper/* branch but then fails
at the PR creation step (e.g., due to permissions), retrying the pipeline
would fail with 422 Unprocessable Entity because the branch already
exists. Now create_branch() checks for and deletes any existing branch
with the same name before creating a fresh one.

fix(deployment): Stop containers before recreating to avoid name conflicts

The --force-recreate flag was causing container name conflicts because docker-compose
was trying to create new containers while the old ones were still running.

Changed approach:
1. docker compose down - Stop and remove all containers
2. docker compose pull - Pull latest images
3. docker compose up -d - Start all services with fresh containers

This ensures clean recreation of all containers without name conflicts.

Strip OpenClaw/Telegram metadata from classification text

OpenClaw prepends conversation metadata (message_id, sender_id,
timestamp) as ```json blocks in user messages. These metadata blocks
contain keywords that pollute the classifier, causing simple messages
like "oi" to classify as "complex" instead of "default".

Added stripMetadata() that removes these known metadata patterns
before keyword matching. Privacy gate still sees original text
(metadata may contain PII that needs detection).

fix: acknowledge DingTalk callback immediately on all paths to prevent redelivery (#392)

Move acknowledge() call before message processing on all code paths
to prevent DingTalk Stream from timing out and redelivering messages.
DingTalk expects callback responses within ~5-15 seconds, but LLM
calls can take 30+ seconds, causing duplicate message processing.

Three paths fixed:
1. Main path (with dedupKey): ack before handleDingTalkMessage()
2. No-dedupKey path: ack before handleDingTalkMessage()

Set max_output_tokens=16384 to prevent truncated Gemini responses

Without response_mime_type (removed because it's incompatible with Google
Search grounding tools), the model returns verbose text-wrapped JSON that
can exceed the default output token limit. Both test articles scored 8.5
ACCEPTED but the JSON was truncated mid-response, causing the parser to
fail with 'Failed to parse Gemini response as JSON'.

deployment) because the SSH command was too large and timing out during docker
pull operations.

Fix parser to only split on known form field labels, not any ### header

The _extract_form_fields parser previously split on every '### ' header in
the issue body. This meant ### subheadings inside the Article Body (like
### Results or ### Discussion) were misinterpreted as new form field
delimiters, silently truncating the article and causing word count failures.

Now the parser only recognizes the exact field labels from submission.yml
(Article Title, Category, Submission Type, Abstract, Artic

fix: install acpx@0.1.16 (pinned version) in extension dir at build time

The base Docker image doesn't ship node_modules for the acpx extension.
OpenClaw tries npm install at startup, but fails with readOnlyRootFilesystem.

Previous fix installed acpx@latest (0.3.1) which caused version mismatch
with the pinned 0.1.16. Now install the exact pinned version at build time.

Global acpx@latest is kept for CLI convenience only.

fix: use OpenClaw built-in acpx instead of overriding with latest

Remove 'npm install acpx@latest' into /app/extensions/acpx/ which overwrote
OpenClaw's pinned acpx 0.1.16 with 0.3.1, causing version mismatch at runtime.
OpenClaw then tried npm install to downgrade, but failed because /home/openclaw/.npm
is not writable (readOnlyRootFilesystem), making ACP unavailable.

Keep the global npm install for CLI convenience only.

fix(race-guard): flock same-shell + tmux field on running + heartbeat task.updated_at (H1+H2+H3)

- 做了什么：为 update-task-status.sh 的 flock 文件描述符增加 EXIT trap，保证同层加锁路径在异常退出时也会释放锁并关闭 FD；dispatch.sh 的 heartbeat 统一复用 update-task-status.sh 刷新 task.updated_at。\n- 遇到的问题：仓库当前实现已经包含 H2/H3 的主体逻辑，本次主要补齐 H1 的锁释放保证，并把 dispatch heartbeat 的调用路径收敛到同一个变量。\n- 有意跳过的内容：未处理 H4/H5 以及 review 文档中记录的其他问题。

Same bug pattern as #11 (lifecycle:error deleting messages) existed in
three other paths where __cleanupStreaming() was called after agent had
been accepted by the server:

- post-acceptance 30min timeout
- cancelSend() after accepted
- sendMessage() catch fallback after accepted

Now these paths use __cleanupTimersAndListeners() + __clearStreamingFlags()
+ __reconcileMessages() instead of __cleanupStreaming(), keeping local

fix: replace git-based registry sync with HTTP tarball download

Remove the hard dependency on git for registry sync. The new approach
downloads the GitHub tarball via HTTP (using ureq) and extracts it with
flate2 + tar, which works in environments without git (Docker containers,
minimal VMs, Android/Termux).

- Primary: HTTP GET of archive tarball, extract with flate2/tar
- Fallback: git clone --depth 1 (for users behind proxies)
- Cache: skip re-download if registry cache is < 24 hours old

fix(safety): escape tool output XML content and remove misleading sanitized attr (#1067)

* fix(safety): escape tool output XML content and remove misleading sanitized attr

The `sanitized="true/false"` attribute on `<tool_output>` misled LLMs into
treating unfiltered content as pre-sanitized. Remove it and add
`escape_xml_content()` to escape `<`, `>`, `&` in tool output body text,
preventing injected XML from breaking the structural boundary.

fix(utils): handle additional Windows OSError codes in process_lock (#854)

On Windows, os.kill(pid, 0) can raise OSError with WinError 11
(ERROR_BAD_FORMAT) in addition to WinError 87 for stale/invalid PIDs.
The previous catch-all OSError handler also silently swallowed errors
on non-Windows platforms. This change:

- Documents both known WinError codes (11, 87) in the comment
- Restricts the broad OSError catch to Windows only (re-raises on
  other platforms to avoid masking real errors)

Move spike binaries out of src/bin/ to fix Tauri bundler

Tauri's bundler tries to package ALL [[bin]] entries in Cargo.toml,
even those gated behind required-features. This caused the release
CI to fail on macOS/Linux because it couldn't find spike_daemon in
the release output.

Fix: moved all spike/test binaries to src-tauri/spike/ directory
(not auto-discovered by Cargo) and removed their [[bin]] entries
from Cargo.toml entirely. To run them:

Android: fix Bitmap memory leaks in PhotosHandler (#41888)

* Android: fix Bitmap memory leaks in PhotosHandler

Bitmaps created by decodeScaledBitmap and intermediate scaled copies
inside encodeJpegUnderBudget were never recycled, leaking native memory
on every photos.latest invocation (up to 20 bitmaps per call).

- latest(): wrap bitmap usage in try/finally to guarantee recycle
- decodeScaledBitmap(): recycle the decoded bitmap after scaling
- encodeJpegUnderBudget(): use try/finally to recyc

🐛 fix(webhook): fetch bot JID from gowa API on startup

After restart, _botJid was null until the bot sent a message and received
its echo. This broke @mention detection in groups with RequireMention=true,
since the mention gate blocked all messages before OpenGroup was checked.
Now lazily fetches the bot JID from gowa's /app/devices endpoint on the
first incoming webhook.

Fix tool call detection: skip action tier when tools are declarative

OpenClaw sends tools[] in every request as "available capabilities",
not as explicit tool-use intent. This was causing all Telegram requests
to classify as "action" (confidence 0.9) instead of reaching domain
detection (complex, analysis, code, etc.).

Now tools in body are ignored for routing — capability-aware upgrade
system handles tool_use requirements independently.

fix(dashboard): stabilize nodeTypes to fix workflow builder editing (#1447)

ReactFlow requires the nodeTypes object to be referentially stable across
renders. The previous implementation included toggleGroup, ungroupNodes,
deleteGroupAndChildren, and the translation function t as useMemo
dependencies, causing nodeTypes to change whenever nodes state changed.
This made ReactFlow unmount/remount all custom nodes on every render,
breaking click handlers (onNodeClick) so users could not open the

## Summary

Tighten shared Vitest fetch cleanup so cross-test fetch leakage is cleaned up without breaking intentional suite-level fetch setup.

## What changed

Updated:
- `test/setup.ts`

The shared test bootstrap now:
- snapshots `globalThis.fetch` at the start of each test
- restores `globalThis.fetch` to that per-test starting value after each test

## Why this patch

A broad macOS CI failure pattern pointed to shared test-state leakage

as a standard OpenClaw plugin with independent versioning and distribution.

- Collaboration: Task FSM, Workflow Engine, Hando

Add gateway self-watchdog and resilience improvements (#29)

* Improve system uptime: rejection resilience, autonomous retry, self-watchdog, startup recovery

- Unhandled rejections: tolerate isolated unknown rejections (threshold 5/60s before crash)
- Autonomous cycle: track consecutive failures with exponential backoff (30s-10min)
- Gateway self-watchdog: periodic health checks trigger SIGUSR1 restart when stuck
- Run-loop: auto-retry startup on non-initial failures with backoff (5 attempts)
